title: R-Exploration
text: Would like to treat this as my notebook/reflection journal after working through new things in R

title: FinleyDibert-Burgwin
text:
Welcome! This website is a place for me to practice my R skills and talk about things that I enjoy while doing so. Consider it a living document—I’ll be adding to it as I learn and go!
A little bit about me—I’m from Silverdale, WA, but I grew up right outside Portland, OR. I LOVE music (Haight-Ashbury was a long awaited bucket-list stop!) and I’ll go to any live show that I can get to. Free time usually means something outdoorsy (diving, snowboarding, foraging), out with my camera, or experimenting with a new syrup combination for my matcha lattes. I also love to watch movies, read, and travel when I get the chance—the past few years have taken me to Hawaii, Mozambique, and more. Below is a bit about my journey at Oregon State and where I’m headed next!

title: FinleyDibert-Burgwin
text:
Oregon State

I came into Oregon State as a declared marine biology major, but in my time here I’ve taken photography courses, line dancing, and more. I fell in love with statistics after I took my first course my junior year; and I immediately looked into a double major. However, I soon realized that while OSU has the only dedicated statistics program in Oregon, it doesn’t offer an undergraduate major—just a minor and graduate program. I declared a minor in statistics and am hoping to earn my master’s after I finish my B.S. this summer. Along with my schoolwork, I’ve enjoyed being a learning assistant for the statistics department. I love working directly with students to facilitate better learning in the classroom, especially in regards to a topic that I’m so passionate about.

title: FinleyDibert-Burgwin
text:
Looking to the future

In the next year, I hope to be accepted to a master’s program in statistics, where I can continue developing my data science skills and strengthening my theoretical foundations. I’m excited to see where the journey takes me!

title: Projects
text:
Predicting House Prices with Machine Learning



Python


Machine Learning


Data Cleaning



This project involves using machine learning algorithms to predict house prices based on various features such as location, size, and amenities. It includes data cleaning, feature engineering, and model selection.



Jan 1, 2024














Customer Segmentation Using Clustering Techniques



R


Machine Learning


Clustering


Statistical Modelling



This project focuses on segmenting customers into different groups based on their purchasing behavior and demographics. It uses clustering algorithms like K-means and hierarchical clustering to identify distinct customer segments.



Apr 1, 2024














Visualizing Global CO2 Emissions



R


Data Visualization


Environmental Science



This project involves creating visualizations to show trends in global CO2 emissions over time. It includes data extraction from public databases, data cleaning, and using visualization libraries to create interactive charts and graphs.



Jul 1, 2024

title: Personal Projects
text:
Project gallery for personal projects related to statistics and data visualization!

Bay-Tabase: 50 Years of San Francisco Bay Water Quality




This Shiny Application was created to display the San Francisco Bay’s water quality data from the United States Geological Survey long-term ecological time series. Click this link to view the app in an external tab.

title: Experience
text:
Work experience
R-Ladies Corvallis | Co-founder | January, 2026 - Present 


Co-founded and helped organize a Corvallis chapter focused on building community among R users and hosting workshops and events for members.


Manage meeting coordination, planning, and outreach.


Oregon State University | Statistics Learning Assistant | September, 2024 - Present


Assisted students in grasping and applying statistical concepts for data-driven problem solving.


Demonstrated techniques and supported assignments using R.


Collaborated with instructors to improve course materials and enhance student learning experiences.



Dutch Bros Coffee | Barista/Shift Lead | May, 2024 - Present


Led shift operations; coordinating team tasks, delegating responsibilities, and ensuring smooth workflow.


Adapted quickly to changing priorities and fast-paced conditions while maintaining strong performance.


Fostered a positive team environment through communication, collaboration, and support.

title: Experience
text:
Relevant Coursework
Statistical Methods I and II (ST 351, ST 352): Study design, descriptive statistics, probability and sampling distributions, hypothesis testing, regression, ANOVA, and logistic regression.
Methods of Data Analysis I and II (ST 411, ST 412): Graphical methods, parametric and nonparametric comparisons, ANOVA, simple and multiple linear regression, model diagnostics, and variable selection.
Data Visualization (ST 437): Visual encoding, design principles, and ethical communication. Emphasis on critiquing and creating publication-ready visualizations.
Mathematical Statistics I and II (ST 421, ST 422): Probability theory, random variables, discrete and continuous distributions, sampling distributions, Central Limit Theorem, estimation, confidence intervals, and hypothesis testing.
Use and Abuse of Data: Critical Thinking in Science (BDS 211): Critical thinking in data analysis — evaluating logical arguments, identifying unstated assumptions, and recognizing deceptive visualizations, misleading comparisons, and spurious reasoning in the context of biological datasets.
Computer Programming (CS 201): Fundamental programming concepts, computational thinking, and algorithmic problem-solving applied to real-world problems.
Foundations of Biological Data Sciences (BDS 310): Scientific computing and data analysis in Python, including data visualization, reproducible research, and applied problem-solving with real-world life sciences datasets.
Applied Conservation Biology in Gorongosa National Park (Mozambique) (ENSC 399): Hands-on fieldwork in ecological data collection, restoration monitoring, and conservation management. Used R to analyze and communicate field data and scientific findings.
Statistical Consulting Practicum (ST 407): Observation of and participation in a statistical consulting practicum, gaining exposure to real-world data problems and client communication.

title: Experience
text:
Volunteer Experience
View Acres Elementary | Classroom Aide | September, 2014 - May, 2020


Logged 500+ hours supporting 4th and 5th grade students in math, reading, and writing.


Worked directly with students during lessons to reinforce concepts and provide individualized support


Assisted teacher with classroom activities and helped maintain an engaging learning environment

title: Stranger Things 5: The Finale
text: They could’ve done so much better. High rating because I love the show and still enjoyed this final season, regardless. But, man, I thought they had more to give. I also have a shit ton of questions. Anyways, I’m sad that this is it after ten years! I definitely cried a few times. One of my favorites with the fam and it’ll always be a classic. 🥲
